q: For an ideal fluid flowing through a pipe, the mass flowing in section 1 must equal the mass flowing in section 2.
The equation of continuity states that the liquid entering one end of the pipe leaves from the other end with a variable volume flow rate.
The larger the area of the pipe, the faster is the speed.
The speed of water spraying from a hose increases by making the hose area smaller.
Bernoulli's equation relates the pressure, flow speed, and height for the steady flow of an ideal compressible fluid.
Bernoulli's equation was first derived in 1738 by the Swiss mathematician Daniel Bernoulli.
According to the work-energy principle, the work done on a section of fluid must equal the change in total mechanical energy.
